reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD and .install files as safe. The PKGBUILD uses pinned checksums for source validation and performs standard font extraction/conversion without dangerous commands. The .install file only displays a license notice.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped reviewing ttf-vista-fonts.cab contents. While binaries are typically skipped, CAB archives can contain executables/scripts. Manual verification confirmed it only contains font files (TTF/TTC).
false_negatives: Neither model considered potential risks from font parsing vulnerabilities (e.g., in fontforge). While low-risk, malicious fonts could exploit parser flaws. This is a systemic limitation in current AI audits.
learnings: Improve binary handling: For archives (CAB/ZIP), attempt extraction and audit contained files if non-binary
Enhance risk awareness: Add checks for known vulnerable tools (e.g., fontforge CVE history) in build chains
Optimize file prioritization: PKGBUILD and install hooks were correctly prioritized; maintain this focus
Reduce verbosity: Both models provided accurate but overly detailed safe verdicts (cost optimization opportunity)
